use_cases.title: Customer support de-escalation
use_cases.why_it_matters: Tone steering can help a base support model sound calmer, more empathetic, and more ownership-oriented without retraining.
use_cases.what_to_notice: The steered reply keeps the same task but sounds more immediate and supportive.
use_cases.user_prompt: A customer says: "My package arrived damaged and I need a replacement before Friday." Reply in two sentences.
use_cases.system_prompt: You are a customer support assistant.
use_cases.baseline: I am so sorry to hear that your package arrived damaged. I can certainly help you arrange for a replacement; could you please provide your order number so I can look into this immediately?
use_cases.steered: I am so sorry to hear that your package arrived damaged; I can certainly help you arrange a replacement right away. Please provide your order number so I can process this for you immediately.

use_cases.title: Encouraging tutoring explanations
use_cases.why_it_matters: Activation steering can nudge a small open model toward clearer, more learner-friendly explanations for classroom or study workflows.
use_cases.what_to_notice: The steered answer becomes more scaffolded and explanatory instead of just terse correctness.
use_cases.user_prompt: A student says: "I do not get why dividing by a fraction means multiplying by its reciprocal." Reply in two sentences.
use_cases.baseline: Think of dividing by a fraction as asking "how many times does this smaller piece fit into the whole?" Multiplying by the reciprocal flips the fraction, which essentially turns the division problem into a multiplication problem that finds that same answer.
use_cases.steered: Think of dividing by a fraction as asking, "How many of this fraction fit into the other number?" To solve this, you flip the divisor to its reciprocal, which turns the division problem into a multiplication problem. This works because the reciprocal represents the inverse relationship needed to find out how many times the smaller piece fits into the whole.

use_cases.title: Risk-aware launch recommendations
use_cases.why_it_matters: Teams can steer the same base model toward more calibrated launch memos, rollout advice, and go-live recommendations.
use_cases.what_to_notice: The steered answer leans harder into phased rollout language and operational validation.
use_cases.user_prompt: Write a two-sentence recommendation on whether to roll out a new billing feature to all users next week after only a small internal beta.
use_cases.system_prompt: You are a product operations assistant.
use_cases.baseline: Given the small internal beta, I recommend a phased rollout, starting with a small segment of users to monitor for unexpected issues before a full launch next week. This approach minimizes risk while still allowing you to gather real-world feedback before impacting the entire user base.
use_cases.steered: **Recommendation:** Proceed with a phased rollout to a small segment of users next week to monitor performance and gather real-world feedback before a full launch. This approach minimizes risk while allowing you to validate the feature's stability and user acceptance at scale.
